steps.title: Spot the contrast clue
steps.content: The sentence says a casual description can make the work seem ______—then the dash introduces the "unimpressive" evidence (coffee shop setting, three women drinking coffee)—but the word "but" reverses it: the mural is actually "complex" and "demands serious attention." The blank must capture how the work sounds at first glance, not how it truly is. It needs to mean modest or low-key.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean modest or understated based on surface description. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — incongruous: means out of place or mismatched — the mural isn’t mismatched with its setting; it just sounds simple. ✗
  • Choice B — shrewd: means clever or calculating — a positive, active quality; nothing suggests cleverness is what the description implies. ✗
  • Choice C — pretentious: means trying to appear more important than it is — the opposite problem; pretentious means overreaching, not underplaying. ✗
  • Choice D — unassuming: means modest, not pretending to be impressive — exactly how a coffee-shop mural of three women drinking coffee would sound on casual description. ✓
That matches Choice D — unassuming.
